title: SENTINEL APEX — 6th July  Threat Intelligence Report
id: 27caabd3-6ff6-5f77-9099-cc6f0b04202d
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to 6th July  Threat Intelligence Report (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: CRITICAL | Class: GENERIC
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/6th July  Threat Intelligence Report
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/07/06
modified: 2026/07/06
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: network_connection
    product: generic
detection:
    selection:
        EventID:
            - 4625
            - 4648
            - 4728
        LogonType: 3
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative access
    - Service account activity
level: critical
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — 6th July  Threat Intelligence Report — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-06T15:20:06Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "6th July  Threat Intelligence Report", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX 6th July  Threat Intelligence Report Exploitation Attempt - GENERIC"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|90 90 90 90|"; rawbytes; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:5285313; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-07-06, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)

items.sigma_rule:
title: SENTINEL APEX — Microsoft Edge Vulnerability Allows Remote Attacker to Execute Arbitrary Code
id: 584c155e-9532-5fd3-aac2-8a1e1fe70a3e
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to Microsoft Edge Vulnerability Allows Remote Attacker to Execute Arbitrary Code (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: LOW | Class: RCE
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/Microsoft Edge Vulnerability Allows Remote Attacker to Execute Arbitrary Code
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/07/06
modified: 2026/07/06
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: process_creation
    product: generic
detection:
    selection_process:
        CommandLine|contains:
            - 'cmd.exe /c'
            - 'powershell -enc'
            - '/bin/sh -c'
            - 'wget http'
            - 'curl http'
    selection_webshell:
        Image|endswith:
            - '\cmd.exe'
            - '\powershell.exe'
            - '/bash'
            - '/sh'
        ParentImage|contains:
            - 'w3wp.exe'
            - 'httpd'
            - 'nginx'
            - 'apache2'
            - 'tomcat'
    condition: selection_process or selection_webshell
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative scripts executing via web server
    - Deployment automation tools
level: low
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — Microsoft Edge Vulnerability Allows Remote Attacker to Execute Arbitrary Code Remote Code Execution Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-06T15:20:06Z
// Deploy to: Microsoft Sentinel / Defender XDR
let lookback = 1d;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where (InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("w3wp.exe", "httpd.exe", "nginx.exe", "tomcat.exe", "java.exe")
    and FileName in~ ("cmd.exe", "powershell.exe", "bash", "sh", "python.exe", "python3"))
    or (ProcessCommandLine has_any ("wget http", "curl http", "certutil -urlcache", "bitsadmin /transfer"))
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine
| where not(ProcessCommandLine has_any ("update", "install", "backup", "deploy"))  // filter known-good
| extend Severity = "HIGH", CVE = "Microsoft Edge Vulnerability Allows Remote Attacker to Execute Arbitrary Code", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by Timestamp desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX Microsoft Edge Vulnerability Allows Remote Attacker to Execute Arbitrary Code Exploitation Attempt - RCE"; flow:established,to_server; content:"/bin/sh"; nocase; content:"cmd.exe"; nocase; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:3552551; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-07-06, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)
items.confidence_rationale: Single source with minimal corroborating evidence
items.recommended_sla_action: MONITOR — Assess in next vulnerability review cycle

items.title: fast-mcp-telegram: Bearer token path traversal bypasses reserved Telegram session protection
items.description:
## Summary

fast-mcp-telegram validates HTTP Bearer tokens by joining the raw token string into a session-file path. The verifier rejects the exact reserved token `telegram`, but it does not reject path separators or normalize the path before checking whether the session file exists. A remote HTTP client can therefore authenticate as the default legacy session with a token such as `../fast-mcp-telegram/telegram` when the documented default session file `~/.config/fast-mcp-telegram/telegram.session` exists.

This bypasses the reserved session name control that is intended to prevent HTTP multi-user sessions from colliding with the default stdio or legacy account. With account-prefixed MCP tools enabled, the attacker still sees and calls the prefixed tools for the default account, so the prefix middleware does not stop the session selection bypass.

## Impact

An unauthenticated network client can access the Telegram account represented by the default `telegram.session` file without know
items.recommended_sla_action: URGENT PATCH — Critical severity, deploy fix within 72 hours
items.apex_ai_summary: [CRITICAL] Vulnerability advisory. Risk score 9.0/10. Full APEX AI analysis available to Pro subscribers.
items._tier_notice: Upgrade to PRO for full enrichment: actor attribution, kill chain, IOC hashes, AI analysis, STIX bundle.

items.title: golang.org/x/image/tiff has excessive resource consumption in PackBits decompression
items.description: The TIFF decoder does not place a limit on the size of PackBits-compressed data. A maliciously-crafted image can exploit this to cause a small image (both in terms of pixel width/height and encoded size) to make the decoder decode large amounts of compressed data.
items.recommended_sla_action: SCHEDULED PATCH — Apply in next maintenance window
items.apex_ai_summary: [HIGH] Vulnerability advisory. Risk score 7.6/10. Full APEX AI analysis available to Pro subscribers.

items.title: Keycloak: Unauthorized access via improper validation of encrypted SAML assertions
items.description: Keycloak's SAML broker endpoint does not properly validate encrypted assertions when the overall SAML response is not signed. An attacker with a valid signed SAML assertion can exploit this by crafting a malicious SAML response, injecting an encrypted assertion for an arbitrary principal, leading to unauthorized access and potential information disclosure.
items.confidence_rationale: Single source with minimal corroborating evidence

items.sigma_rule:
title: SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-44937 - SUSE Rancher Fleet had an Unauthenticated Webhook Regex Injecti
id: b82b3c15-0d53-581a-9ae9-f6f57a658c6e
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2026-44937 (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: LOW | Class: GENERIC
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-44937
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/07/06
modified: 2026/07/06
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: network_connection
    product: generic
detection:
    selection:
        EventID:
            - 4625
            - 4648
            - 4728
        LogonType: 3
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative access
    - Service account activity
level: low
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-44937 — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-06T15:20:06Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "CVE-2026-44937", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2026-44937 Exploitation Attempt - GENERIC"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|90 90 90 90|"; rawbytes; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:3747143; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-07-06, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)
